context.pr_body:
## Problem
The `translate_with_backoff` method in `EPUBBookLoaderHelper` class was raising TypeError because of parameter mismatch between the method signature and actual calls through the backoff decorator.

Error message:
TypeError: EPUBBookLoaderHelper.translate_with_backoff() takes 1 positional argument but 3 were given

## Solution
1. Modified the method signature to properly handle parameters:
   - Added explicit parameters for text and context_flag
   - Added jitter=None to backoff decorator
   
2. Improved error handling and logging
#bug

### README

[![litellm](https://img.shields.io/badge/%20%F0%9F%9A%85%20liteLLM-OpenAI%7CAzure%7CAnthropic%7CPalm%7CCohere%7CReplicate%7CHugging%20Face-blue?color=green)](https://github.com/BerriAI/litellm)

# bilingual_book_maker
The bilingual_book_maker is an AI translation tool that uses ChatGPT to assist users in creating multi-language versions of epub/txt/srt files and books. This tool is exclusively designed for translating epub books that have entered the public domain and is not intended for copyrighted works. Before using this tool, please review the project's **[disclaimer](./disclaimer.md)**.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15976103/222317531-a05317c5-4eee-49de-95cd-04063d9539d9.png)

## Supported Models
gpt-4, gpt-3.5-turbo, claude-2, palm, llama-2, azure-openai, command-nightly, gemini
For using Non-OpenAI models, use class `liteLLM()` - liteLLM supports all models above.
Find more info here for using liteLLM: https://github.com/BerriAI/litellm/blob/main/setup.py

## Preparation

1. ChatGPT or OpenAI token [^token]
2. epub/txt books
3. Environment with internet access or proxy
4. Python 3.8+

## Use

- `pip install -r requirements.txt` or `pip install -U bbook_maker`(you can use)
- Use `--openai_key` option to specify OpenAI API key. If you have multiple keys, separate them by commas (xxx,xxx,xxx) to reduce errors caused by API call limits.
   Or, just set environment variable `BBM_OPENAI_API_KEY` instead.
- A sample book, `test_books/animal_farm.epub`, is provided for testing purposes.
- The default underlying model is [GPT-3.5-turbo](https://openai.com/blog/introducing-chatgpt-and-whisper-apis), which is used by ChatGPT currently. Use `--model gpt4` to change the underlying model to `GPT4`. You can also use `GPT4omini`.
  - Important to note that `gpt-4` is significantly more expensive than `gpt-4-turbo`, but to avoid bumping into rate limits, we automatically balance queries across `gpt-4-1106-preview`, `gpt-4`, `gpt-4-32k`, `gpt-4-0613`,`gpt-4-32k-0613`.
    - If you want to use a specific model alias with OpenAI (eg `gpt-4-1106-preview` or `gpt-3.5-turbo-0125`), you can use `--model openai --model_list gpt-4-1106-preview,gpt-3.5-turbo-0125`. `--model_list` takes a comma-separated list of model aliases.
  - If using chatgptapi, you can add `--use_context` to add a context paragraph to each passage sent to the model for translation (see below).
- Support DeepL model [DeepL Translator](https://rapidapi.com/splintPRO/api/dpl-translator) need pay to get the token use `--model deepl --deepl_key ${deepl_key}`
- Support DeepL free model `--model deeplfree`
- Support Google [Gemini](https://aistudio.google.com/app/apikey) model, use `--model gemini` for Gemini Flash or `--model geminipro` for Gemini Pro. `--gemini_key ${gemini_key}`
    - If you want to use a specific model alias with Gemini (eg `gemini-1.5-flash-002` or `gemini-1.5-flash-8b-exp-0924`), you can use `--model gemini --model_list g

### File Overview
This file provides EPUBBookLoaderHelper — a small helper class to insert translations into parsed EPUB content and to call the underlying translation model with retry/backoff. It also contains several utility functions for detecting links, figure/list captions, ISBNs, and other text patterns used to decide whether to translate a text node.

### Architecture
- EPUBBookLoaderHelper encapsulates state needed for translating elements (translate_model, accumulated_num, translation_style, context_flag) and provides methods to insert translation nodes and to coordinate batched translations.
- translate_with_backoff is the single retried wrapper around the underlying translate_model.translate call; it is decorated with backoff.on_exception to perform exponential retry on exceptions.
- deal_new and deal_old manage when to flush/insert translations for a new tag versus a waiting list of tags; insert_trans performs the DOM manipulation (uses copy of node, sets .string, optionally sets style, inserts after original, and can remove the original when single_translate is True).
- Utility functions (is_text_link, is_text_tail_link, shorter_result_link, is_text_source, is_text_list, is_text_figure, is_text_digit_and_space, is_text_isbn, not_trans) live at module level and are used to detect texts that should not be translated or to shorten strings containing URLs.

### Functions & Classes
- EPUBBookLoaderHelper — class encapsulating translation helper logic and state.
  - __init__(self, translate_model, accumulated_num, translation_style, context_flag) — stores translate_model and configuration values. Important because translate_model is the abstraction used for actual translation operations and other methods rely on these stored values.
  - insert_trans(self, p, text, translation_style="", single_translate=False) — inserts a translated copy of a node `p` after `p` with its .string set to `text`. If `translation_style` is provided, sets the new node's "style" attribute. If `single_translate` is True, removes the original node (p.extract()). Also avoids inserting when text is None or identical (ignoring spaces) to existing p.string. Important for performing DOM updates safely and avoiding duplicate/no-op inserts.
  - translate_with_backoff(self, **kwargs) — wrapper around self.translate_model.translate(**kwargs) that is decorated with:
      @backoff.on_exception(
          backoff.expo,
          Exception,
          on_backoff=lambda details: logger.warning(f"retry backoff: {details}"),
          on_giveup=lambda details: logger.warning(f"retry abort: {details}"),
      )
    This is the method responsible for retrying translation when translate_model.translate raises Exception; the backoff uses exponential backoff and logs warnings on each backoff and on giveup. Signature is currently def translate_with_backoff(self, **kwargs). Important: this is the retried translation entrypoint used elsewhere in this class.
  - deal_new(self, p, wait_p_list, single_translate=False) — calls self.deal_old(wait_p_list, single_translate, self.context_flag) to flush pending translations, then calls self.insert_trans with the translation of the new node `p`. The call used is:
      shorter_result_link(self.translate_with_backoff(p.text, self.context_flag))
    Important because it demonstrates how translate_with_backoff is invoked (with positional args p.text and self.context_flag).
  - deal_old(self, wait_p_list, single_translate=False, context_flag=False) — if wait_p_list is empty returns; otherwise obtains translations via self.translate_model.translate_list(wait_p_list) (called directly, not via translate_with_backoff), inserts translated texts for each p in wait_p_list using shorter_result_link, then clears wait_p_list. Important because it handles batch translations and shows translate_model also provides translate_list.

- url_pattern — module-level regex string used by several helpers to detect URLs.

- is_text_link(text) — returns True if text matches url_pattern at the start (uses re.compile(url_pattern).match). Important for detecting standalone link texts.

- is_text_tail_link(text, num=80) — returns True if a URL appears at the end of text and the entire text length is less than num. Important for excluding texts where only trailing URL exists.

- shorter_result_link(text, num=20) — finds first url_pattern match in text and replaces it with "..." if the URL is longer than num; otherwise returns original text. Important for shortening translation results that include long URLs before inserting into DOM.

- is_text_source(text) — returns True if text.strip().startswith("Source: "). Used to avoid translating source lines.

- is_text_list(text, num=80) — returns True if text matches r"^Listing\s*\d+" and length < num. Used to skip translating listing captions.

- is_text_figure(text, num=80) — returns True if text matches r"^Figure\s*\d+" and length < num. Used to skip translating figure captions.

- is_text_digit_and_space(s) — returns True if string contains only digits and whitespace. Used to skip pure-numeric lines (page numbers, chapter numbers).

- is_text_isbn(s) — returns True if string matches r"^[Ee]?ISBN\s*\d[\d\s]*$". Used to avoid translating ISBN lines.

- not_trans(s) — returns True if any of the above detection functions report that s should not be translated. Central helper to decide translation eligibility.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
The agent specifically wanted to inspect EPUBBookLoaderHelper and translate_with_backoff signature and how the backoff decorator is used.

- translate_with_backoff is decorated with backoff.on_exception(backoff.expo, Exception, ...) and logs warnings on backoff and on giveup — so the backoff configuration is explicit and present in this file.
- The signature of translate_with_backoff is def translate_with_backoff(self, **kwargs): return self.translate_model.translate(**kwargs).
- There is a likely mismatch: translate_with_backoff is called in deal_new as self.translate_with_backoff(p.text, self.context_flag) — that passes positional arguments (p.text and self.context_flag). Since translate_with_backoff only accepts keyword arguments (**kwargs) besides self, calling it with positional arguments will raise a TypeError at runtime. This is the probable root cause if the agent observed a signature/decorator mismatch.
- Additional relevant points: deal_old calls translate_model.translate_list(wait_p_list) directly (no backoff wrapper), so batch translations do not use the backoff retry wrapper; only the single-item translation path in deal_new attempts to use translate_with_backoff (but does so with incompatible call style).

tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
This file defines the EPUBBookLoader class which reads an EPUB, iterates its document items and translation-relevant tags, and produces a bilingual EPUB by translating paragraphs. It orchestrates paragraph batching, resume/save state, test mode, and delegates actual translation and retry/backoff logic to the provided translate_model and to EPUBBookLoaderHelper.

### Architecture
- EPUBBookLoader extends BaseBookLoader and is responsible for EPUB I/O, parsing HTML content via BeautifulSoup, extracting translation target elements, batching paragraphs based on token counts, and inserting translated text back into the parsed HTML.
- Translation execution is performed by the translate_model instance (passed in via the constructor). Helper operations and some orchestration (especially for grouped paragraph handling) are delegated to EPUBBookLoaderHelper (constructed in the loader).
- Two translation flows exist:
  - Immediate/synchronous translation via translate_model.translate (also translate_model.add_to_batch_translate_queue / translate_model.batch_translate for batch mechanisms).
  - Batched/accumulated paragraph translation via translate_paragraphs_acc which uses EPUBBookLoaderHelper.deal_new and EPUBBookLoaderHelper.deal_old to handle paragraphs too large individually or to combine paragraphs for single/batched requests.
- The loader supports resume via pickle of p_to_save, writing temporary bilingual EPUBs, retranslate (fix a subrange from an existing bilingual EPUB), and optional batch translation lifecycle management (batch_init_then_wait and invoking translate_model.batch()).

### Functions & Classes
- EPUBBookLoader — main class; constructor and many methods below.

Constructor: __init__(self, epub_name, model, key, resume, language, model_api_base=None, is_test=False, test_num=5, prompt_config=None, single_translate=False, context_flag=False, temperature=1.0, context_paragraph_limit=0)
- Purpose: initialize loader state, create translate_model instance, create EPUBBookLoaderHelper, monkey-patch ebooklib writer/reader behaviors for compatibility, open origin EPUB, and optionally load resume state.
- Importance: sets up translate_model and helper that will be used throughout; controls single_translate / batch flags and context behavior.

_static method_ _is_special_text(text)
- Purpose: return True if text is digits, whitespace, a link (via is_text_link), or only punctuation.
- Importance: used to skip paragraphs that should not be translated.

_make_new_book(self, book)
- Purpose: create a new epub.EpubBook with metadata, spine, and toc copied from input book.
- Importance: used to construct the bilingual output book.

_extract_paragraph(self, p)
- Purpose: remove excluded sub-tags (from self.exclude_translate_tags) from a paragraph before translation.
- Importance: ensures excluded elements (e.g., sup) are not fed to translators or appear in translation blocks.

_process_paragraph(self, p, new_p, index, p_to_save_len)
- Purpose: Translate a single paragraph according to current batching flags and resume state; insert translated content back via helper.insert_trans; update p_to_save and save progress periodically.
- Behavior:
  - If resume and previously saved translation exists for this index, reuses it.
  - If self.batch_flag: add to translate_model.add_to_batch_translate_queue(index, new_p.text).
  - elif self.batch_use_flag: call translate_model.batch_translate(index) and use returned text.
  - else: call translate_model.translate(new_p.text).
  - After obtaining t_text, writes it into new_p or p, appends to p_to_save, and calls self.helper.insert_trans(p, new_p.string, self.translation_style, self.single_translate).
- Importance: central synchronous (per-paragraph) translation path used when accumulated_num == 1 and not using translate_paragraphs_acc.

_process_combined_paragraph(self, p_block, index, p_to_save_len)
- Purpose: Combine multiple paragraphs into a single translation request (join with newlines), call translate_model.translate on the joint text, split returned lines, and insert translations back via helper.insert_trans.
- Importance: used for single_translate with block_size to reduce calls and preserve context across multiple consecutive paragraphs.

translate_paragraphs_acc(self, p_list, send_num)
- Purpose: Accumulate paragraphs into groups constrained by token count (send_num) and invoke EPUBBookLoaderHelper.deal_new / EPUBBookLoaderHelper.deal_old as appropriate.
- Behavior and uses of helper:
  - Iterates paragraphs; for special/empty/ignored paragraphs it may call self.helper.deal_old(wait_p_list, self.single_translate) when reaching the last paragraph.
  - If an individual paragraph's token length > send_num it calls self.helper.deal_new(p, wait_p_list, self.single_translate).
  - Uses num_tokens_from_text(temp_p.text) to count tokens; builds wait_p_list and calls deal_old or deal_new when accumulated length crosses send_num.
- Importance: This is the key method that delegates grouping and actual translation of accumulated paragraphs to EPUBBookLoaderHelper; all calls to EPUBBookLoaderHelper.deal_new and EPUBBookLoaderHelper.deal_old originate here.

get_item(self, book, name)
- Purpose: Return an item by file_name from a book.
- Importance: utility used in retranslate_book.

find_items_containing_string(self, book, search_string)
- Purpose: Finds document items whose content contains search_string.
- Importance: used in retranslate_book auto-discovery.

retranslate_book(self, index, p_to_save_len, pbar, trans_taglist, retranslate)
- Purpose: Load an already-translated bilingual EPUB and retranslate a specified range between fixstart and fixend by copying the corresponding original paragraphs into the target file and reprocessing that item via process_item.
- Importance: supports partial correction of already produced bilingual ebooks.

has_nest_child(self, element, trans_taglist)
- Purpose: Recursively check whether an element has a child whose name is in trans_taglist or contains such nested children.
- Importance: used to avoid translating nested tags multiple times.

filter_nest_list(self, p_list, trans_taglist)
- Purpose: Filter out parent elements that have nested translatable children; retains only leaf nodes for translation.
- Importance: ensures paragraphs are not translated multiple times due to nesting.

process_item(self, item, index, p_to_save_len, pbar, new_book, trans_taglist, fixstart=None, fixend=None)
- Purpose: Process a single EPUB item (document), locate paragraphs/tags to translate, optionally slice for retranslate ranges, and either call translate_paragraphs_acc (when accumulated mode) or translate each paragraph via _process_paragraph/_process_combined_paragraph.
- Important behaviors:
  - Honors only_filelist and exclude_filelist.

### File Overview
This file defines the EPUBBookLoader class which reads an EPUB, iterates its document items and translation-relevant tags, and produces a bilingual EPUB by translating paragraphs. It orchestrates paragraph batching, resume/save state, test mode, and delegates actual translation and retry/backoff logic to the provided translate_model and to EPUBBookLoaderHelper.

### Architecture
- EPUBBookLoader extends BaseBookLoader and is responsible for EPUB I/O, parsing HTML content via BeautifulSoup, extracting translation target elements, batching paragraphs based on token counts, and inserting translated text back into the parsed HTML.
- Translation execution is performed by the translate_model instance (passed in via the constructor). Helper operations and some orchestration (especially for grouped paragraph handling) are delegated to EPUBBookLoaderHelper (constructed in the loader).
- Two translation flows exist:
  - Immediate/synchronous translation via translate_model.translate (also translate_model.add_to_batch_translate_queue / translate_model.batch_translate for batch mechanisms).
  - Batched/accumulated paragraph translation via translate_paragraphs_acc which uses EPUBBookLoaderHelper.deal_new and EPUBBookLoaderHelper.deal_old to handle paragraphs too large individually or to combine paragraphs for single/batched requests.
- The loader supports resume via pickle of p_to_save, writing temporary bilingual EPUBs, retranslate (fix a subrange from an existing bilingual EPUB), and optional batch translation lifecycle management (batch_init_then_wait and invoking translate_model.batch()).

- Purpose: initialize loader state, create translate_model instance, create EPUBBookLoaderHelper, monkey-patch ebooklib writer/reader behaviors for compatibility, open origin EPUB, and optionally load resume state.

_static method_ _is_special_text(text)
- Purpose: return True if text is digits, whitespace, a link (via is_text_link), or only punctuation.

- Purpose: create a new epub.EpubBook with metadata, spine, and toc copied from input book.

- Purpose: remove excluded sub-tags (from self.exclude_translate_tags) from a paragraph before translation.

_process_paragraph(self, p, new_p, index, p_to_save_len)
- Purpose: Translate a single paragraph according to current batching flags and resume state; insert translated content back via helper.insert_trans; update p_to_save and save progress periodically.
- Behavior:
  - If resume and previously saved translation exists for this index, reuses it.
  - If self.batch_flag: add to translate_model.add_to_batch_translate_queue(index, new_p.text).
  - elif self.batch_use_flag: call translate_model.batch_translate(index) and use returned text.
  - else: call translate_model.translate(new_p.text).

tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
This file provides helper utilities for loading and translating EPUB content. The core is class EPUBBookLoaderHelper which wraps a translation model with backoff retry behavior and helpers to insert translated text into parsed document nodes; the file also includes many small textual heuristics (is_text_*) and a URL-shortening helper (shorter_result_link).

### Architecture
- Top-level imports and a logger are defined, then a class EPUBBookLoaderHelper encapsulates translation behavior and DOM insertion logic for paragraphs/nodes.
- EPUBBookLoaderHelper holds a translate_model (expected to expose translate and translate_list), and exposes methods to insert translated text, to call the model with retry/backoff, and to process both new single paragraphs and accumulated lists.
- Several independent helper functions (is_text_link, is_text_tail_link, shorter_result_link, is_text_source, is_text_list, is_text_figure, is_text_digit_and_space, is_text_isbn, not_trans) and a url_pattern regex are used to decide whether to skip translation and to shorten long URL-containing results.

### Functions & Classes
- EPUBBookLoaderHelper — class that coordinates translation calls and insertion of translation results into document nodes.
  - __init__(self, translate_model, accumulated_num, translation_style, context_flag) — stores translate_model, accumulated_num, translation_style, and context_flag on the instance. Important because translate_model is used to perform translations and the flags control translation style and context usage.
  - insert_trans(self, p, text, translation_style="", single_translate=False) — inserts translated text into a parsed node p:
    - If text is None, normalizes to empty string.
    - Compares p.string (after stripping spaces) with text to avoid duplicate insertion.
    - Uses copy(p) to create new_p, sets new_p.string = text, optionally sets new_p["style"] = translation_style.
    - Calls p.insert_after(new_p) and if single_translate True, calls p.extract().
    - Important because it handles the DOM manipulation for adding translations to the document (assumes p has .string, .insert_after, .extract — likely a BeautifulSoup Tag or similar).
  - translate_with_backoff — decorated with backoff.on_exception and defined as:
    - @backoff.on_exception(
        backoff.expo,
        Exception,
        on_backoff=lambda details: logger.warning(f"retry backoff: {details}"),
        on_giveup=lambda details: logger.warning(f"retry abort: {details}"),
      )
      def translate_with_backoff(self, **kwargs):
          return self.translate_model.translate(**kwargs)
    - This wraps translate_model.translate with exponential backoff on Exception. Signature is def translate_with_backoff(self, **kwargs). It forwards kwargs to self.translate_model.translate(**kwargs).
    - Very important to the agent's goal: this is the implementation and exact signature you are locating; note it accepts only keyword arguments and forwards them as keywords to translate.
  - deal_new(self, p, wait_p_list, single_translate=False) — processes a "new" paragraph p:
    - Calls self.deal_old(wait_p_list, single_translate, self.context_flag) first to flush any queued paragraphs.
    - Calls self.insert_trans(p, shorter_result_link(self.translate_with_backoff(p.text, self.context_flag)), self.translation_style, single_translate)
    - Important because it is the caller of translate_with_backoff; it calls translate_with_backoff with positional arguments (p.text, self.context_flag) rather than as keyword args, which will produce a signature mismatch with translate_with_backoff(self, **kwargs).
  - deal_old(self, wait_p_list, single_translate=False, context_flag=False) — processes accumulated paragraphs:
    - If wait_p_list is empty, returns.
    - Calls result_txt_list = self.translate_model.translate_list(wait_p_list).
    - Iterates over wait_p_list and if result exists for index i, inserts translated text via insert_trans(p, shorter_result_link(result_txt_list[i]), ...).
    - Clears wait_p_list at the end.
    - Important because it bypasses translate_with_backoff and calls translate_list directly (bulk translation path).
- url_pattern — variable holding regex string for URL detection. Used by the helper functions below.
- is_text_link(text) — returns True if text (stripped) matches url_pattern at start using re.compile(...).match. Used to detect that the text is a link and should probably not be translated.
- is_text_tail_link(text, num=80) — strips text, checks if the text ends with a URL (pattern with url_pattern anchored at end) and text length < num. Used to detect short lines ending with a URL.
- shorter_result_link(text, num=20) — finds the first URL in text; if not found or the matched URL length < num returns original text; otherwise replaces occurrences of url_pattern with "..." and returns the shortened string. Used when inserting translation results that contain long URLs.
- is_text_source(text) — returns True if text.strip().startswith("Source: "). Detects source lines that likely should not be translated.
- is_text_list(text, num=80) — returns True if text matches r"^Listing\s*\d+" and length < num. Detects listing captions.
- is_text_figure(text, num=80) — returns True if text matches r"^Figure\s*\d+" and length < num. Detects figure captions.
- is_text_digit_and_space(s) — returns True if every character in s is digit or whitespace. Detects numeric-only lines like page numbers.
- is_text_isbn(s) — returns True if s matches r"^[Ee]?ISBN\s*\d[\d\s]*$". Detects ISBN lines.
- not_trans(s) — returns True if any of the above detectors (is_text_link, is_text_tail_link, is_text_source, is_text_list, is_text_figure, is_text_digit_and_space, is_text_isbn) returns True. Used to decide whether to skip translation.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- You asked to locate translate_with_backoff implementation and exact signature to identify a mismatch and lines to modify. translate_with_backoff is implemented inside class EPUBBookLoaderHelper with exact signature:
  - def translate_with_backoff(self, **kwargs):
      return self.translate_model.translate(**kwargs)
  - It is decorated with @backoff.on_exception(...) to retry on Exception.
- The call site causing a signature mismatch is in method deal_new:
  - self.insert_trans(
        p,
        shorter_result_link(self.translate_with_backoff(p.text, self.context_flag)),
        self.translation_style,
        single_translate,
    )
  - Here translate_with_backoff is invoked with two positional arguments (p.text, self.context_flag) although translate_with_backoff only accepts keyword arguments (**kwargs) and forwards them as keywords to translate_model.translate.
- Lines to consider modifying:
  - Either change the call in deal_new to pass keyword arguments matching what translate_model.translate expects (for example, translate_with_backoff(text=p.text, context_flag=self.context_flag) if those are the expected parameter names), or change translate_with_backoff signature to accept positional arguments (for example def translate_with_backoff(self, *args, **kwargs): return self.translate_model.translate(*args, **kwargs)).
- Also note that deal_old calls translate_model.translate_list(wait_p_list) directly (bulk translation) and does not use translate_with_backoff; if you need retry/backoff for list translations you may also need to add a backoff-wrapped wrapper for translate_list.

- url_pattern — variable holding regex string for URL detection. Used by the helper functions below.

- is_text_tail_link(text, num=80) — strips text, checks if the text ends with a URL (pattern with url_pattern anchored at end) and text length < num. Used to detect short lines ending with a URL.
- shorter_result_link(text, num=20) — finds the first URL in text; if not found or the matched URL length < num returns original text; otherwise replaces occurrences of url_pattern with "..." and returns the shortened string. Used when inserting translation results that contain long URLs.
- is_text_source(text) — returns True if text.strip().startswith("Source: "). Detects source lines that likely should not be translated.
- is_text_list(text, num=80) — returns True if text matches r"^Listing\s*\d+" and length < num. Detects listing captions.
- is_text_figure(text, num=80) — returns True if text matches r"^Figure\s*\d+" and length < num. Detects figure captions.

### Architecture
- Single abstract class `Base` organizes translator behavior.
- Initialization sets up a cycling key iterator and a `language` attribute.
- Two abstract methods (`rotate_key`, `translate`) specify the required interface for subclasses.
- A concrete no-op method `set_deployment_id` is provided for optional overriding by subclasses.

  - `__init__(self, key, language) -> None` — constructor. It creates `self.keys` as an `itertools.cycle` over `key.split(",")` (i.e., cycles through comma-separated keys forever). It also stores `self.language = language`. Important because it defines how keys are provided and exposes the `language` value translators should use.
  - `rotate_key(self)` — abstract method; no implementation here. Subclasses must implement rotation behavior (presumably advance or change the key used from `self.keys` or manage other rotation logic). Important because callers relying on key rotation expect this method to exist.

import re
import time
import os
import shutil
from copy import copy
from os import environ
from itertools import cycle
import json

from openai import AzureOpenAI, OpenAI, RateLimitError
from rich import print

from .base_translator import Base
from ..config import config

CHATGPT_CONFIG = config["translator"]["chatgptapi"]

PROMPT_ENV_MAP = {
    "user": "BBM_CHATGPTAPI_USER_MSG_TEMPLATE",
    "system": "BBM_CHATGPTAPI_SYS_MSG",
}

GPT35_MODEL_LIST = [
    "gpt-3.5-turbo",
    "gpt-3.5-turbo-1106",
    "gpt-3.5-turbo-16k",
    "gpt-3.5-turbo-0613",
    "gpt-3.5-turbo-16k-0613",
    "gpt-3.5-turbo-0301",
    "gpt-3.5-turbo-0125",
]
GPT4_MODEL_LIST = [
    "gpt-4-1106-preview",
    "gpt-4",
    "gpt-4-32k",
    "gpt-4o-2024-05-13",
    "gpt-4-0613",
    "gpt-4-32k-0613",
]

GPT4oMINI_MODEL_LIST = [
    "gpt-4o-mini",
    "gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18",
]
GPT4o_MODEL_LIST = [
    "gpt-4o",
    "gpt-4o-2024-05-13",
    "gpt-4o-2024-08-06",
    "chatgpt-4o-latest",
]


class ChatGPTAPI(Base):
    DEFAULT_PROMPT = "Please help me to translate,`{text}` to {language}, please return only translated content not include the origin text"

    def __init__(
        self,
        key,
        language,
        api_base=None,
        prompt_template=None,
        prompt_sys_msg=None,
        temperature=1.0,
        context_flag=False,
        context_paragraph_limit=0,
        **kwargs,
    ) -> None:
        super().__init__(key, language)
        self.key_len = len(key.split(","))
        self.openai_client = OpenAI(api_key=next(self.keys), base_url=api_base)
        self.api_base = api_base

        self.prompt_template = (
            prompt_template
            or environ.get(PROMPT_ENV_MAP["user"])
            or self.DEFAULT_PROMPT
        )
        self.prompt_sys_msg = (
            prompt_sys_msg
            or environ.get(
                "OPENAI_API_SYS_MSG",
            )  # XXX: for backward compatibility, deprecate soon
            or environ.get(PROMPT_ENV_MAP["system"])
            or ""
        )
        self.system_content = environ.get("OPENAI_API_SYS_MSG") or ""
        self.deployment_id = None
        self.temperature = temperature
        self.model_list = None
        self.context_flag = context_flag
        self.context_list = []
        self.context_translated_list = []
        if context_paragraph_limit > 0:
            # not set by user, use default
            self.context_paragraph_limit = context_paragraph_limit
        else:
            # set by user, use user's value
            self.context_paragraph_limit = CHATGPT_CONFIG["context_paragraph_limit"]
        self.batch_text_list = []
        self.batch_info_cache = None
        self.result_content_cache = {}

    def rotate_key(self):
        self.openai_client.api_key = next(self.keys)

    def rotate_model(self):
        self.model = next(self.model_list)

    def create_messages(self, text, intermediate_messages=None):
        content = self.prompt_template.format(
            text=text, language=self.language, crlf="\n"
        )

        sys_content = self.system_content or self.prompt_sys_msg.format(crlf="\n")
        messages = [
            {"role": "system", "content": sys_content},
        ]

        if intermediate_messages:
            messages.extend(intermediate_messages)

        messages.append({"role": "user", "content": content})
        return messages

    def create_context_messages(self):
        messages = []
        if self.context_flag:
            messages.append({"role": "user", "content": "\n".join(self.context_list)})
            messages.append(
                {
                    "role": "assistant",
                    "content": "\n".join(self.context_translated_list),
                }
            )
        return messages

    def create_chat_completion(self, text):
        messages = self.create_messages(text, self.create_context_messages())
        completion = self.openai_client.chat.completions.create(
            model=self.model,
            messages=messages,
            temperature=self.temperature,
        )
        return completion

    def get_translation(self, text):
        self.rotate_key()
        self.rotate_model()  # rotate all the model to avoid the limit

        completion = self.create_chat_completion(text)

        # TODO work well or exception finish by length limit
        # Check if content is not None before encoding
        if completion.choices[0].message.content is not None:
            t_text = completion.choices[0].message.content.encode("utf8").decode() or ""
        else:
            t_text = ""

        if self.context_flag:
            self.save_context(text, t_text)

        return t_text

    def save_context(self, text, t_text):
        if self.context_paragraph_limit > 0:
            self.context_list.append(text)
            self.context_translated_list.append(t_text)
            # Remove the oldest context
            if len(self.context_list) > self.context_paragraph_limit:
                self.context_list.pop(0)
                self.context_translated_list.pop(0)

    def translate(self, text, needprint=True):
        start_time = time.time()
        # todo: Determine whether to print according to the cli option
        if needprint:
            print(re.sub("\n{3,}", "\n\n", text))

        attempt_count = 0
        max_attempts = 3
        t_text = ""

        while attempt_count < max_attempts:
            try:
                t_text = self.get_translation(text)
                break
            except RateLimitError as e:
                # todo: better sleep time? why sleep alawys about key_len
                # 1. openai server error or own network interruption, sleep for a fixed time
                # 2. an apikey has no money or reach limit, don`t sleep, just replace it with another apikey
                # 3. all apikey reach limit, then use current sleep
                sleep_time = int(60 / self.key_len)
                print(e, f"will sleep {sleep_time} seconds")
                time.sleep(sleep_time)
                attempt_count += 1
                if attempt_count == max_attempts:
                    print(f"Get {attempt_count} consecutive exceptions")
                    raise
            except Exception as e:
                print(str(e))
                return

        # todo: Determine whether to print according to the cli option
        if needprint:
            print("[bold green]" + re.sub("\n{3,}", "\n\n", t_text) + "[/bold green]")

        time.time() - start_time
        # print(f"translation time: {elapsed_time:.1f}s")

        return t_text

    def translate_and_split_lines(self, text):
        result_str = self.translate(text, False)
        lines = result_str.splitlines()
        lines = [line.strip() for line in lines if line.strip() != ""]
        return lines

    def get_best_result_list(
        self,
        plist_len,
        new_str,
        sleep_dur,
        result_list,
        max_retries=15,
    ):
        if len(result_list) == plist_len:
            return result_list, 0

        best_result_list = result_list
        retry_count = 0

        while retry_count < max_retries and len(result_list) != plist_len:
            print(
                f"bug: {plist_len} -> {len(result_list)} : Number of paragraphs before and after translation",
            )
            print(f"sleep for {sleep_dur}s and retry {retry_count+1} ...")
            time.sleep(sleep_dur)
            retry_count += 1
            result_list = self.translate_and_split_lines(new_str)
            if (
                len(result_list) == plist_len
                or len(best_result_list) < len(result_list) <= plist_len
                or (
                    len(result_list) < len(best_result_list)
                    and len(best_result_list) > plist_len
                )
            ):
                best_result_list = result_list

        return best_result_list, retry_count

    def log_retry(self, state, retry_count, elapsed_time, log_path="log/buglog.txt"):
        if retry_count == 0:
            return
        print(f"retry {state}")
        with open(log_path, "a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            print(
                f"retry {state}, count = {retry_count}, time = {elapsed_time:.1f}s",
                file=f,
            )

    def log_translation_mismatch(
        self,
        plist_len,
        result_list,
        new_str,
        sep,
        log_path="log/buglog.txt",
    ):
        if len(result_list) == plist_len:
            return
        newlist = new_str.split(sep)
        with open(log_path, "a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            print(f"problem size: {plist_len - len(result_list)}", file=f)
            for i in range(len(newlist)):
                print(newlist[i], file=f)
                print(file=f)
                if i < len(result_list):
                    print("............................................", file=f)
                    print(result_list[i], file=f)
                    print(file=f)
                print("=============================", file=f)

        print(
            f"bug: {plist_len} paragraphs of text translated into {len(result_list)} paragraphs",
        )
        print("continue")

    def join_lines(self, text):
        lines = text.splitlines()
        new_lines = []
        temp_line = []

        # join
        for line in lines:
            if line.strip():
                temp_line.append(line.strip())
            else:
                if temp_line:
                    new_lines.append(" ".join(temp_line))
                    temp_line = []
                new_lines.append(line)

        if temp_line:
            new_lines.append(" ".join(temp_line))

        text = "\n".join(new_lines)
        # try to fix #372
        if not text:
            return ""

        # del ^M
        text = text.replace("^M", "\r")
        lines = text.splitlines()
        filtered_lines = [line for line in lines if line.strip() != "\r"]
        new_text = "\n".join(filtered_lines)

        return new_text

    def translate_list(self, plist):
        sep = "\n\n\n\n\n"
        # new_str = sep.join([item.text for item in plist])

        new_str = ""
        i = 1
        for p in plist:
            temp_p = copy(p)
            for sup in temp_p.find_all("sup"):
                sup.extract()
            new_str += f"({i}) {temp_p.get_text().strip()}{sep}"
            i = i + 1

        if new_str.endswith(sep):
            new_str = new_str[: -len(sep)]

        new_str = self.join_lines(new_str)

        plist_len = len(plist)

        print(f"plist len = {len(plist)}")

        result_list = self.translate_and_split_lines(new_str)

        start_time = time.time()

        result_list, retry_count = self.get_best_result_list(
            plist_len,
            new_str,
            6,  # WTF this magic number here?
            result_list,
        )

        end_time = time.time()

        state = "fail" if len(result_list) != plist_len else "success"
        log_path = "log/buglog.txt"

        self.log_retry(state, retry_count, end_time - start_time, log_path)
        self.log_translation_mismatch(plist_len, result_list, new_str, sep, log_path)

        # del (num), num. sometime (num) will translated to num.
        result_list = [re.sub(r"^(\(\d+\)|\d+\.|(\d+))\s*", "", s) for s in result_list]
        return result_list

    def set_deployment_id(self, deployment_id):
        self.deployment_id = deployment_id
        self.openai_client = AzureOpenAI(
            api_key=next(self.keys),
            azure_endpoint=self.api_base,
            api_version="2023-07-01-preview",
            azure_deployment=self.deployment_id,
        )

    def set_gpt35_models(self, ollama_model=""):
        if ollama_model:
            self.model_list = cycle([ollama_model])
            return
        # gpt3 all models for save the limit
        if self.deployment_id:
            self.model_list = cycle(["gpt-35-turbo"])
        else:
            my_model_list = [
                i["id"] for i in self.openai_client.models.list().model_dump()["data"]
            ]
            model_list = list(set(my_model_list) & set(GPT35_MODEL_LIST))
            print(f"Using model list {model_list}")
            self.model_list = cycle(model_list)

    def set_gpt4_models(self):
        # for issue #375 azure can not use model list
        if self.deployment_id:
            self.model_list = cycle(["gpt-4"])
        else:
            my_model_list = [
                i["id"] for i in self.openai_client.models.list().model_dump()["data"]
            ]
            model_list = list(set(my_model_list) & set(GPT4_MODEL_LIST))
            print(f"Using model list {model_list}")
            self.model_list = cycle(model_list)

    def set_gpt4omini_models(self):
        # for issue #375 azure can not use model list
        if self.deployment_id:
            self.model_list = cycle(["gpt-4o-mini"])
        else:
            my_model_list = [
                i["id"] for i in self.openai_client.models.list().model_dump()["data"]
            ]
            model_list = list(set(my_model_list) & set(GPT4oMINI_MODEL_LIST))
            print(f"Using model list {model_list}")
            self.model_list = cycle(model_list)

    def set_gpt4o_models(self):
        # for issue #375 azure can not use model list
        if self.deployment_id:
            self.model_list = cycle(["gpt-4o"])
        else:
            my_model_list = [
                i["id"] for i in self.openai_client.models.list().model_dump()["data"]
            ]
            model_list = list(set(my_model_list) & set(GPT4o_MODEL_LIST))
            print(f"Using model list {model_list}")
            self.model_list = cycle(model_list)

    def set_model_list(self, model_list):
        model_list = list(set(model_list))
        print(f"Using model list {model_list}")
        self.model_list = cycle(model_list)

    def batch_init(self, book_name):
        self.book_name = self.sanitize_book_name(book_name)

    def add_to_batch_translate_queue(self, book_index, text):
        self.batch_text_list.append({"book_index": book_index, "text": text})

    def sanitize_book_name(self, book_name):
        # Replace any characters that are not alphanumeric, underscore, hyphen, or dot with an underscore
        sanitized_book_name = re.sub(r"[^\w\-_\.]", "_", book_name)
        # Remove leading and trailing underscores and dots
        sanitized_book_name = sanitized_book_name.strip("._")
        return sanitized_book_name

    def batch_metadata_file_path(self):
        return os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "batch_files", f"{self.book_name}_info.json")

    def batch_dir(self):
        return os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "batch_files", self.book_name)

    def custom_id(self, book_index):
        return f"{self.book_name}-{book_index}"

    def is_completed_batch(self):
        batch_metadata_file_path = self.batch_metadata_file_path()

        if not os.path.exists(batch_metadata_file_path):
            print("Batch result file does not exist")
            raise Exception("Batch result file does not exist")

        with open(batch_metadata_file_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            batch_info = json.load(f)

        for batch_file in batch_info["batch_files"]:
            batch_status = self.check_batch_status(batch_file["batch_id"])
            if batch_status.status != "completed":
                return False

        return True

    def batch_translate(self, book_index):
        if self.batch_info_cache is None:
            batch_metadata_file_path = self.batch_metadata_file_path()
            with open(batch_metadata_file_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                self.batch_info_cache = json.load(f)

        batch_info = self.batch_info_cache
        target_batch = None
        for batch in batch_info["batch_files"]:
            if batch["start_index"] <= book_index < batch["end_index"]:
                target_batch = batch
                break

        if not target_batch:
            raise ValueError(f"No batch found for book_index {book_index}")

        if target_batch["batch_id"] in self.result_content_cache:
            result_content = self.result_content_cache[target_batch["batch_id"]]
        else:
            batch_status = self.check_batch_status(target_batch["batch_id"])
            if batch_status.output_file_id is None:
                raise ValueError(f"Batch {target_batch['batch_id']} is not completed")
            result_content = self.get_batch_result(batch_status.output_file_id)
            self.result_content_cache[target_batch["batch_id"]] = result_content

        result_lines = result_content.text.split("\n")
        custom_id = self.custom_id(book_index)
        for line in result_lines:
            if line.strip():
                result = json.loads(line)
                if result["custom_id"] == custom_id:
                    return result["response"]["body"]["choices"][0]["message"][
                        "content"
                    ]

        raise ValueError(f"No result found for custom_id {custom_id}")

    def create_batch_context_messages(self, index):
        messages = []
        if self.context_flag:
            if index % CHATGPT_CONFIG[
                "batch_context_update_interval"
            ] == 0 or not hasattr(self, "cached_context_messages"):
                context_messages = []
                for i in range(index - 1, -1, -1):
                    item = self.batch_text_list[i]
                    if len(item["text"].split()) >= 100:
                        context_messages.append(item["text"])
                        if len(context_messages) == self.context_paragraph_limit:
                            break

                if len(context_messages) == self.context_paragraph_limit:
                    print("Creating cached context messages")
                    self.cached_context_messages = [
                        {"role": "user", "content": "\n".join(context_messages)},
                        {
                            "role": "assistant",
                            "content": self.get_translation(
                                "\n".join(context_messages)
                            ),
                        },
                    ]

            if hasattr(self, "cached_context_messages"):
                messages.extend(self.cached_context_messages)

        return messages

    def make_batch_request(self, book_index, text):
        messages = self.create_messages(
            text, self.create_batch_context_messages(book_index)
        )
        return {
            "custom_id": self.custom_id(book_index),
            "method": "POST",
            "url": "/v1/chat/completions",
            "body": {
                # model shuould not be rotate
                "model": self.batch_model,
                "messages": messages,
                "temperature": self.temperature,
            },
        }

    def create_batch_files(self, dest_file_path):
        file_paths = []
        # max request 50,000 and max size 100MB
        lines_per_file = 40000
        current_file = 0

        for i in range(0, len(self.batch_text_list), lines_per_file):
            current_file += 1
            file_path = os.path.join(dest_file_path, f"{current_file}.jsonl")
            start_index = i
            end_index = i + lines_per_file

            # TODO: Split the file if it exceeds 100MB
            with open(file_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                for text in self.batch_text_list[i : i + lines_per_file]:
                    batch_req = self.make_batch_request(
                        text["book_index"], text["text"]
                    )
                    json.dump(batch_req, f, ensure_ascii=False)
                    f.write("\n")
            file_paths.append(
                {
                    "file_path": file_path,
                    "start_index": start_index,
                    "end_index": end_index,
                }
            )

        return file_paths

    def batch(self):
        self.rotate_model()
        self.batch_model = self.model
        # current working directory
        batch_dir = self.batch_dir()
        batch_metadata_file_path = self.batch_metadata_file_path()
        # cleanup batch dir and result file
        if os.path.exists(batch_dir):
            shutil.rmtree(batch_dir)
        if os.path.exists(batch_metadata_file_path):
            os.remove(batch_metadata_file_path)
        os.makedirs(batch_dir, exist_ok=True)
        # batch execute
        batch_files = self.create_batch_files(batch_dir)
        batch_info = []
        for batch_file in batch_files:
            file_id = self.upload_batch_file(batch_file["file_path"])
            batch = self.batch_execute(file_id)
            batch_info.append(
                self.create_batch_info(
                    file_id, batch, batch_file["start_index"], batch_file["end_index"]
                )
            )
        # save batch info
        batch_info_json = {
            "book_id": self.book_name,
            "batch_date": time.strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"),
            "batch_files": batch_info,
        }
        with open(batch_metadata_file_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            json.dump(batch_info_json, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)

    def create_batch_info(self, file_id, batch, start_index, end_index):
        return {
            "input_file_id": file_id,
            "batch_id": batch.id,
            "start_index": start_index,
            "end_index": end_index,
            "prefix": self.book_name,
        }

    def upload_batch_file(self, file_path):
        batch_input_file = self.openai_client.files.create(
            file=open(file_path, "rb"), purpose="batch"
        )
        return batch_input_file.id

    def batch_execute(self, file_id):
        current_time = time.strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
        res = self.openai_client.batches.create(
            input_file_id=file_id,
            endpoint="/v1/chat/completions",
            completion_window="24h",
            metadata={
                "description": f"Batch job for {self.book_name} at {current_time}"
            },
        )
        if res.errors:
            print(res.errors)
            raise Exception(f"Batch execution failed: {res.errors}")
        return res

    def check_batch_status(self, batch_id):
        return self.openai_client.batches.retrieve(batch_id)

    def get_batch_result(self, output_file_id):
        return self.openai_client.files.content(output_file_id)

### File Overview
This file implements ChatGPTAPI, a concrete translator class that uses OpenAI/AzureOpenAI chat completions to translate text. It provides single-text translation (translate), batch translation support (translate_list and numerous batch_* methods), and optional contextual translation controlled by a context_flag.

### Architecture
- Top-level configuration/constants (CHATGPT_CONFIG, PROMPT_ENV_MAP, GPT* model lists) configure prompts and allowed models.
- The ChatGPTAPI class inherits from Base and encapsulates API client management (OpenAI/AzureOpenAI), key/model rotation, prompt/message construction, translation execution, context management, and batch translation flows.
- translate and translate_list are the two primary user-facing translation entry points. translate delegates to get_translation which does API calls; translate_list joins many paragraphs into one request, calls translate_and_split_lines, and post-processes results.
- Context support is integrated into message construction via create_context_messages (single requests) and create_batch_context_messages (batch requests), and maintained in instance lists via save_context.

### Functions & Classes
- CHATGPT_CONFIG — module-level config extracted from ..config; contains translator-specific settings used by ChatGPTAPI, e.g., "context_paragraph_limit" and "batch_context_update_interval".
- PROMPT_ENV_MAP — mapping of prompt roles to environment variable names (keys: "user", "system").
- GPT35_MODEL_LIST, GPT4_MODEL_LIST, GPT4oMINI_MODEL_LIST, GPT4o_MODEL_LIST — lists of model ids used to select available models.

- ChatGPTAPI (class) — main translator implementation; inherits Base.
  - DEFAULT_PROMPT — default user prompt template string used if none provided.
  - __init__(self, key, language, api_base=None, prompt_template=None, prompt_sys_msg=None, temperature=1.0, context_flag=False, context_paragraph_limit=0, **kwargs) — constructor.
    - Responsibilities: initializes OpenAI client, sets prompt templates (prompt_template, prompt_sys_msg), handles system_content, sets temperature, context_flag, context_paragraph_limit, and various caches (context_list, context_translated_list, batch_text_list, result_content_cache).
    - Important: Accepts context_flag (bool) and context_paragraph_limit (int) — indicates the translator supports context and that the agent can pass context_flag when creating an instance.
  - rotate_key(self) — rotates API key by setting openai_client.api_key = next(self.keys). Important for key rotation between requests.
  - rotate_model(self) — rotates current model by setting self.model = next(self.model_list). Used before requests to spread load across models.
  - create_messages(self, text, intermediate_messages=None) — builds the messages array for a chat completion: a system message (system_content or prompt_sys_msg) + optional intermediate_messages + a user message created from prompt_template.format(text=text, language=self.language, crlf="\n"). This is central to how the translator prompts the model.
  - create_context_messages(self) — if self.context_flag is True, returns a list with a user content message joining self.context_list and an assistant content message joining self.context_translated_list; otherwise returns empty list. This is how contextual conversation history is injected into single-request translations.
  - create_chat_completion(self, text) — creates messages via create_messages(text, create_context_messages()) and calls self.openai_client.chat.completions.create(model=self.model, messages=messages, temperature=self.temperature). Returns the completion object.
  - get_translation(self, text) — rotates key and model (rotate_key, rotate_model), calls create_chat_completion(text), extracts completion.choices[0].message.content safely (handles None), decodes to string, calls save_context(text, t_text) if context_flag is set, and returns the translated text. This is the low-level single-API-call translator used by translate.
  - save_context(self, text, t_text) — if context_paragraph_limit > 0 appends text and t_text to self.context_list and self.context_translated_list and enforces the context_paragraph_limit by popping oldest entries. Used to maintain in-memory context for subsequent requests.
  - translate(self, text, needprint=True) — public single-text translation entry point. Signature: translate(self, text, needprint=True). Behavior:
    - Optionally prints the source (needprint).
    - Retries up to max_attempts (3) calling get_translation, handling RateLimitError (sleeps int(60 / self.key_len) and rotates keys via rotate_key triggered in get_translation) and other exceptions.
    - Optionally prints the translated text.
    - Returns the translated string (t_text) or returns None on non-RateLimit exception (it logs and returns).
    - This is the primary method the agent expects; it supports an optional needprint parameter.
  - translate_and_split_lines(self, text) — helper that calls translate(text, False), splits result into non-empty stripped lines and returns that list. Used by translate_list to break the combined translation back into paragraph units.
  - get_best_result_list(self, plist_len, new_str, sleep_dur, result_list, max_retries=15) — attempts retries when translate_and_split_lines returns an unexpected number of paragraphs; sleeps sleep_dur between attempts; keeps best_result_list based on several heuristics; returns (best_result_list, retry_count). Used by translate_list to improve robustness when splitting/parsing yields mismatched paragraph counts.
  - log_retry(self, state, retry_count, elapsed_time, log_path="log/buglog.txt") — logs retry information if retry_count > 0.
  - log_translation_mismatch(self, plist_len, result_list, new_str, sep, log_path="log/buglog.txt") — logs mismatches between expected list length and result_list to a buglog file for debugging.
  - join_lines(self, text) — normalizes lines: joins contiguous non-empty lines into single paragraphs, removes ^M artifacts, filters stray carriage-only lines. Used by translate_list to prepare the request string.
  - translate_list(self, plist) — signature: translate_list(self, plist). Behavior and importance:
    - Given a list-like plist (in code it expects objects with .get_text() and .find_all("sup") — likely BeautifulSoup Tag objects), constructs a single large string with a fixed separator sep = "\n\n\n\n\n" and prefixes each paragraph with an index like "(1) ".
    - Removes <sup> tags from copies, joins via sep, normalizes via join_lines, prints plist length.
    - Calls translate_and_split_lines(new_str) to get an initial result_list, then calls get_best_result_list to retry if lengths mismatch.
    - Post-processes results to remove leading numbering patterns via regex re.sub(r"^(\(\d+\)|\d+\.|(\d+))\s*", "", s).
    - Returns result_list (list of translated paragraph strings). This method provides multi-paragraph/batched translation in a single model call and is the translate_list the agent expects.
  - set_deployment_id(self, deployment_id) — assigns self.deployment_id and reinitializes self.openai_client to AzureOpenAI with azure_endpoint self.api_base, api_version "2023-07-01-preview", and azure_deployment self.deployment_id. Important for Azure usage and changes how models are selected/used.
  - set_gpt35_models(self, ollama_model="") — populates self.model_list (an itertools.cycle) with GPT-3.5 candidate models or a single provided ollama_model. If deployment_id is set, uses ["gpt-35-turbo"]. Uses self.openai_client.models.list() when not Azure to intersect with GPT35_MODEL_LIST.
  - set_gpt4_models(self) — similar to set_gpt35_models but for GPT4_MODEL_LIST; uses ["gpt-4"] when deployment_id is set.
  - set_gpt4omini_models(self) — similar but for GPT4oMINI_MODEL_LIST; uses ["gpt-4o-mini"] when deployment_id is set.
  - set_gpt4o_models(self) — similar but for GPT4o_MODEL_LIST; uses ["gpt-4o"] when deployment_id is set.
  - set_model_list(self, model_list) — de-duplicates the provided model_list, prints it, and sets self.model_list = cycle(model_list).
  - batch_init(self, book_name) — stores sanitized book_name via sanitize_book_name into self.book_name.
  - add_to_batch_translate_queue(self, book_index, text) — appends {"book_index": book_index, "text": text} to self.batch_text_list for later batch creation.
  - sanitize_book_name(self, book_name) — returns a sanitized filename-safe book name by replacing non-alphanumeric/_-/. chars with _, and trimming leading/trailing . and _. Used by batch_init for file naming.
  - batch_metadata_file_path(self) — returns path to batch metadata JSON: os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "batch_files", f"{self.book_name}_info.json").
  - batch_dir(self) — returns path to batch dir: os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "batch_files", self.book_name).
  - custom_id(self, book_index) — returns f"{self.book_name}-{book_index}" used to correlate batch responses to inputs.
  - is_completed_batch(self) — reads batch metadata file and checks each batch file's status via check_batch_status; returns True only if all are "completed". Raises if metadata missing.
  - batch_translate(self, book_index) — retrieves cached batch_info (or loads metadata file), finds the batch that contains book_index, obtains or fetches batch result (get_batch_result), parses its JSON lines, finds the line with matching custom_id and returns the translation content at result["response"]["body"]["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]. Raises if not found. This is the per-item retrieval path for batch jobs.
  - create_batch_context_messages(self, index) — constructs contextual messages for batch requests if context_flag is True:
    - It updates a cached_context_messages property every CHATGPT_CONFIG["batch_context_update_interval"] indices (or when not present) by gathering up to context_paragraph_limit preceding long paragraphs (>=100 words) and calling get_translation on their joined text to form an assistant message. If cached_context_messages exists it is returned. This supports context when sending batch jobs built offline.
  - make_batch_request(self, book_index, text) — builds the body for a single line in a jsonl batch file. Uses self.batch_model (set during batch()) and create_batch_context_messages(book_index) to add context messages. Returns a dict with custom_id, method, url, and body for the batch system.
  - create_batch_files(self, dest_file_path) — splits self.batch_text_list into files with lines_per_file = 40000, writes jsonl files where each line is a JSON batch request from make_batch_request. Returns list of file metadata dicts with file_path, start_index, end_index.
  - batch(self) — orchestrates batch job creation and submission:
    - rotate_model to pick batch_model, prepares batch directory and metadata, uses create_batch_files to write files, uploads each via upload_batch_file, executes batches via batch_execute, composes and writes batch metadata JSON to batch_metadata_file_path.
  - create_batch_info(self, file_id, batch, start_index, end_index) — returns dict with input_file_id, batch_id, start_index, end_index, prefix self.book_name. Used by batch to assemble metadata.
  - upload_batch_file(self, file_path) — calls self.openai_client.files.create(file=open(file_path, "rb"), purpose="batch") and returns file id. (Interacts with OpenAI SDK.)
  - batch_execute(self, file_id) — calls self.openai_client.batches.create(...) to create a batch job; raises if res.errors. Returns the batch result object.
  - check_batch_status(self, batch_id) — returns self.openai_client.batches.retrieve(batch_id).
  - get_batch_result(self, output_file_id) — returns self.openai_client.files.content(output_file_id).

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- translate signature: translate(self, text, needprint=True) — this is the expected single-text method. It calls get_translation internally and returns a translated string. The method supports optional needprint boolean.
- translate_list support: translate_list(self, plist) exists and is implemented. It expects plist items with .get_text() and .find_all("sup") (i.e., BeautifulSoup-like Tag objects), builds a combined request with a large separator, calls translate_and_split_lines to get lines, retries with get_best_result_list to match paragraph counts, and strips injected numbering before returning a list of translated paragraphs.
- Context support: context_flag is explicitly supported via the __init__ parameter (context_flag=False by default) and influences behavior in:
  - save_context(self, text, t_text) — stores recent pairs when context_flag is True and context_paragraph_limit > 0.
  - create_context_messages(self) — injects stored context into single-request messages.
  - create_batch_context_messages(self, index) — builds cached context messages for batch requests (calls get_translation on context paragraphs to get assistant content).
  - The agent can enable context by constructing ChatGPTAPI with context_flag=True and optionally set context_paragraph_limit.
- Low-level API call flow: get_translation -> create_chat_completion -> openai_client.chat.completions.create(...). For Azure usage, set_deployment_id replaces the client with AzureOpenAI and model selection methods handle deployment differences.
- Error handling & retries: translate contains retry logic for RateLimitError and general exceptions; get_best_result_list provides extra retries for paragraph splitting mismatches. This is important if you plan to alter retry behavior or expected outputs.

If you need to change the translate API signature (e.g., add context_flag parameter per-call instead of constructor), relevant places to modify are:
- __init__ (context_flag currently stored on the instance),
- create_context_messages and create_batch_context_messages (they check self.context_flag),
- translate, get_translation, and save_context (they rely on self.context_flag and context storage).
